_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging key soft commodities coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice and set to 100 in 2015, currently stands at 141, reflecting about a 41% increase since 2015. This rise is not uniform across the basket, with some items climbing much more than others. The increase has been relatively recent and uneven, highlighting volatility within the component prices. Overall, the basket signals significant but selective inflation in breakfast-related commodities.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have nearly doubled since 2015, rising by 99%, making it the largest contributor to the basket’s increase. Orange juice prices are up 34%, wheat by 19%, and sugar by 13%. These price movements largely stem from weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought impacting coffee and citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice. Soft commodities typically trade in thin markets, meaning smaller supply disruptions can sharply influence global prices, rather than reflecting broad-based inflation.
notes.why: The headline inflation in this basket is driven by a handful of weather-exposed soft commodities where supply shocks are amplified. Because these crops are sensitive to climate and disease, prices can remain elevated for years if adverse conditions persist. This dynamic underscores the nature of soft commodity markets as particularly susceptible to environmental factors rather than generalized economic trends.
